titleStmt.title: SII 16.332: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswami and G. S. Gai
No. 332. (A.R. No. 80 of 1912.) KOḌIGĒPALLE, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a slab built into the wall of the ruined maṇḍapa near the Āñjanēya temple.
This is dated Śaka 1567, Pārthiva, Āśvīja śu. 4, corresponding to 1645 A.D., September 14.

The inscription records that Ōbaḷarāya Kōnēṭi-nāyaniṅgāru, son of Kastūrināyanigāru repaired the shrine and consecrated the deity in the temple of Pāla-Veṅkaṭēśvara in Dinnamīdi-Koḍigēpalle alias Pālaveṅkaṭāpuram in Guyyulūrunāḍu included in Penugoṇḍa-durga with the consent of Peda-Veṅkaṭapatirāya who seems to have made some provisions (details lost) for offerings and worship in the temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī vijayābhyudaya śālivā
hana śakavarṣaṁbbulu 1567 Aguna
nēṭi pārththivasaṁvatsara Āśvīja śu 4 śrīmadrā
jādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratā
pa śrīvīra śrīraṁggarāyadēvamahārāya
layyavāru pṛthvisāmrājyaṁ sēyucu
nuṁḍagānu lakkamanāyanigāri pautṛ
laina kastūrināyanigāri putrulaina
[Obha]ḷarāya kōnēṭināyanigāru Akhi
lāṁḍakōṭibraṁhmāṁḍanāyaku
laina pālaveṁkaṭēśvara svāmulaku sa
marppiṁcivunna penugoṁḍadurgānaku
celē guyyulūrunāḍu dinnamīdi ko
ḍigipalleku pratināmamaisa pālaveṁkaṭa
puragrāmānaku vēsina silāśāsana vi
varaṁ pedaveṁkaṭapatirāyalayyavāri
Appaṇamīdanu mēmu penugoṁḍadu
rggaṁ yellalaku celle prākugrāmāna saṁvva
tsara māgha śu 15 lu mā kulasvāmiyai
na pālaveṁkaṭēśvarasvāmula jīrṇōddhā
raṁsēsi kōvila gōpura
navi kaṭṭiṁcci yī svāmi
Utsava mūtturla pratiṣṭhākālamaṁdu mā
yēlina svāmula
lavidha kaiṁkaryamulunnu naḍacēṭaṭṭu
gānu yī penugoṁḍa
koḍigi palliku pra
tināmamaina pālaveṁkaṭāpura grā
mānaku The following lines are engraved on the back of the slab.
śubhamastu . śrīśrīśrī
veṁkaṭēśvara . svastiśrī vi-
jayābhyudaya śālivāha-
na śakava¨rṣaṁbulu 1567 Agu
nanēṭi pā¨rtivasaṁvatsara Aśvi-
ja śu 10 śrīmadrājādhirāja
Digital edition of SII 16.332 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
